scores.brief_assessment: An exceptionally well-structured reference that provides complete, copy-paste-ready boilerplate for both example and test files, documents a proprietary comparison library API with scenario-to-method tables and ellipsis pattern conventions, and covers every step of the grove-create workflow from file placement to literalinclude syntax — with minor redundancy between the inline test code comment and the dedicated Comparison API section.
scores.novel_info: The most novel elements are the internal `Utilities.Comparison` fluent API — specifically the `Expect.That()` / `ShouldMatch()` / `ShouldResemble()` / `WithSchema()` method chain, the mutual-exclusivity rules between those methods, and the `WithIgnoredFields` / `WithOrderedSort` / `WithUnorderedSort` modifiers — which appear to be a bespoke in-repo library with no public equivalent. The ellipsis pattern conventions used in expected output files (`"..."`, `"prefix..."`, `["..."]`, standalone `...`, `{ ... }`) and the `snip.js` extraction pipeline that produces `YourExample.snippet.your-snippet-name.cs` files under `content/code-examples/tested/` are also organization-specific conventions that a human reviewer should verify against the actual source code in `code-example-tests/csharp/driver/Utilities/Comparison/` and the `snip.js` implementation.
